ཚེས་གྲངས་དེ་དག་ལ་མེ་ལྷ་མི་ཡུལ་དུ་བཞུགས་པས། སྦྱིན་སྲེག་འབུལ་ན་ཕན་ཡོན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ཆེ།
On these dates, the Fire Deity abides in the human realm. Making fire offerings (Jinsek) brings great benefits.
NOTE: On other dates, the Fire Deity abides in the god realm or naga realm, so fire offerings should NOT be made.

raw_rows.col3: It is improper to host guests or pursue matters of great importance.
It is improper to begin or launch any kind of business enterprise.
It is improper to hold birth celebrations or take a child out of the house for the first time.
It is improper to initiate military actions or start legal disputes and lawsuits.
It is improper to hold banquets or parties for relatives and kin.
It is improper to begin building projects, perform renovations, or move to a new home.
It is improper to send or receive a bride (marriage ceremonies).
It is improper to perform burials or establish tombs.
It is improper to conduct virtuous deeds (merit-making) or feasts for the deceased.
It is improper to engage in general activities, and especially to hold important consultations or meetings.

raw_rows.col1: These are the auspicious times for each day of the week according to Tibetan astrology. Use these times for important activities, ceremonies, and auspicious beginnings.

daily_calendar_english.Auspicious Day's Short Desc: The start of the Tibetan lunar calendar, traditionally observed with prayers, offerings, and aspirations for prosperity, harmony, and spiritual growth.
daily_calendar_english.Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Happy Losar!
As the new year dawns, may all beings be blessed with happiness, peace, and spiritual awakening. May the coming year bring auspiciousness, good health, longevity, and the fulfillment of all virtuous wishes. Tashi Delek!
daily_calendar_english.Element Meaning: Increasing factors for ample food & clothing. Good for giving generously, sowing seeds, practice of Vasudhārā.
daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Tibetan New Year (Losar)
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Om! To the deities of the wondrous and auspicious realms,
I prostrate with devotion; please hold me with your compassion.
May the two benefits be spontaneously accomplished, and marvelous auspiciousness arise.
May we be enriched by the glory of virtue, excellence, benefit, and bliss.
May the unchanging Tree of Enlightenment always flourish.
May all thoughts in accordance with the Dharma be fulfilled!
Samaya. This was transcribed onto miraculous paper in the small script of [Yeshe] Tsogyal.
May it meet with the destined emanation of the Dro-mi (nomad) through auspicious coincidence.
This is from the profound Dharma of Sangdak Dorje Dradul (also known as Mingyur Dorje), the heart-son of Garwang Dudjom Lingpa.
daily_calendar_english.2nd Event: Lama Losar (New Year of the Guru)
daily_calendar_english.2nd Event's Details:
The first day is the most significant day, representing faith and auspiciousness.
* Waking up early: People rise before dawn, dress in their finest traditional clothes (Chuba), and perform incense offerings (Sang-sol) to the deities.
* Fetching the First Water: Very early in the morning, the "first water" (Chu-phud) is fetched from a spring or well to be placed on the altar as an offering.
* Making Offerings and Paying Respects: Families gather to enjoy traditional foods like Tsampa and Droma Dresi (sweet buttered rice with small tubers). Afterward, they visit monasteries and temples to pray and make offerings.
* Family Gathering: According to tradition, the first day is reserved for immediate family. People generally stay home together rather than visiting relatives or friends on this day.
Preparations Before the New Year
Before Losar begins, the following items must be prepared:
* Chemar and Droso Chemar: Preparing the auspicious "five-grain bucket" (Chemar) filled with roasted barley flour, butter, and seeds, decorated with colorful butter sculptures.
* Making Khapse: Frying various shapes of traditional Tibetan deep-fried pastries (Khapse).
* House Cleaning: Thoroughly cleaning the entire house to sweep away the bad luck of the previous year.
* New Clothes: Making or buying new clothes for the whole family to wear during the celebrations.

daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:

daily_calendar_english.Element Meaning: Increasing factors for ample food & clothing. Good for giving generously, sowing seeds, practice of Vasudhārā.
daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Gyalpo Losar (King's New Year)
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
This day is dedicated to visiting and exchanging greetings with relatives and friends.
* Visiting Relatives: People start going out to visit the homes of relatives, friends, and teachers to celebrate the New Year together.
* Tashi Delek (Auspicious Greetings): People offer each other ceremonial scarves (Khata), present the auspicious Chemar (barley flour and butter offering), and offer traditional barley beer (Chang) as a welcome.
* Enjoying Feasts: Large meals are hosted where guests enjoy a variety of Tibetan dishes, particularly traditional Tibetan butter tea and barley beer, in a joyful atmosphere.

daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:

— Dudjom Rinpoche
daily_calendar_english.Element Meaning: Coincidence of burning, generate suffering. Will bring war & suffering to others.
daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Incense Offering (Sangsol) at the local Tenkhar
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
The Source of Happiness and Fortune
A Concise Smoke Offering to Gesar
by Sera Khandro
 
om ah hung
oṃ āḥ hūṃ
zakmé yeshe dütsi sang chö di
I present this smoke offering of pure wisdom nectar
rik sum sempé gyutrul sengchen gyal
To the Great Lion, King Gesar, magical emanation of the bodhisattvas of the Three Families,
kakhor lejé sungmé tsok la bul
And to the assembly of servants and guardians.
zhé la sampé dön kün drubpar dzö
Please accept it and accomplish all our wishes.
 
Thus, prompted by laziness, this concise text was written by the one named Dewé Dorje during the accumulation of a specific number of recitations.
daily_calendar_english.2nd Event: Chos-Kyong Losar (New Year of the Protectors)
daily_calendar_english.2nd Event's Details:
This is the day dedicated to making offerings to the Dharma Protectors and accumulating merit.
* Changing Prayer Flags: People hoist new prayer flags (Dar-chog) on their rooftops and mountain tops. They also perform elaborate incense burning ceremonies (Sang-sol).
* Chanting "Lha-Gyal-Lo": Large groups of people gather to shout "Kye Kye Lha Gyal Lo!" (Victory to the Deities!), throwing roasted barley flour into the air as an auspicious prayer for success and protection.
* Festivities and Entertainment: From this day onward, public celebrations increase with traditional folk songs, dances, and various forms of community entertainment.

daily_calendar_english.Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
daily_calendar_english.Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Loden Choksé
On the tenth day of the dragon month, you are ordained by Ānanda,
And with countless scholars and adepts who have achieved the ultimate in knowledge,
You attain consummate mastery over all the sūtras and tantras,
And are famed as both Shakya Senge and Loden Choksé—at your feet we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that the lords of death, planetary demons and the eight classes who bring harm all are pacified,
Pure discipline and the three higher trainings are perfected,
And our knowledge of every field of learning, and of all the sūtras and tantras,
Grows to become limitless.

"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: The Audience of the Eight Manifestations of Guru Rinpoche Cham Dance
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
The sacred Cham dance of the Eight Manifestations of Guru Rinpoche, arising from the lineage of Dechen Lingpa and Dakla Gampo’s Ogyen Drodrul Lingpa, is known to possess a spiritual power and blessing far greater than others. Below is a brief explanation of its history and benefits.
Characteristics of the Lineage
This lineage stems from a continuous succession of Pure Visions (Daknang) and Revealed Treasures (Terma). Therefore, the movements of the dance, the melodies, and the costumes are said to be in perfect accordance with the enlightened intention of Guru Rinpoche himself.
Benefits of Witnessing the Dance
Within the non-sectarian traditions of Tibetan Buddhism, this Cham dance is referred to as "Liberation through Seeing" (Thongdrol), meaning that simply beholding it with one’s eyes has the power to plant the seed of liberation.
* Clearing All Obstacles: As the dance is inseparable from the actual presence of Guru Rinpoche, it has the benefit of averting obstacles caused by humans and non-humans, as well as warding off epidemics and untimely death.
* Purification of Negativities: If witnessed with one-pointed faith, the negative karma and obscurations accumulated since beginningless lifetimes will be purified.
* Receiving Blessings in One’s Being: Each movement of the Eight Manifestations creates the auspicious connection for the five poisons to be liberated into their natural state and for primordial wisdom to arise within the mind-stream.
* Rebirth in the Pure Land: Through the merit of witnessing this dance, one establishes a profound connection to be reborn in the copper-colored mountain pure land (Zangdok Palri) in the future.

daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Chimé Pemajungné
On the tenth day of the snake month, the king of Zahor
Burns you in a fire, which you transform into a lake, emerging resplendent upon the heart of a lotus blossom.
Relying on Princess Mandāravā as consort, you attain the vajra body—
Immortal Lotus-born, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that all harm is eliminated from ill-omened planets and stars, from enemies and thieves,
When at home or abroad, auspiciousness, prosperity and well-being increase,
Well-intentioned guardians act always as our allies and support,
And all our wishes are spontaneously fulfilled.

daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Pema Vajra Tsal
On the tenth day of the horse month, when the malicious ministers of Uḍḍiyāna
Burn you alive, you display the amazing miracle of remaining unscathed,
And, by teaching the Kadü Chökyi Gyatso, lead the king and his entourage to enlightenment—
Padma Vajra Tsal, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that all harm from māras, asuras, vicious earth lords and nāgas is completely eliminated,
And they obey the instructions they are given,
Dharmapālas and guardians carry out all our commands,
And patron gods afford their protection.

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Saga Dawa: Tibetan Vesak Day
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Saga Dawa is one of the most significant and blessed festivals in Tibetan Buddhism. Here is a translation of the details you provided:
1. Significance of the Festival
The name ""Saga"" comes from one of the 28 constellations in traditional astrology. On the 15th day of this month, the full moon coincides with the Saga star, hence the name ""Saga Dawa"" (Saga Month). This month is especially sacred as it marks a ""triple-blessed"" occasion: the Buddha's birth, his enlightenment, and his Parinirvana (passing away).
2. Historical Events
* Birth: The anniversary of Buddha Shakyamuni’s birth in the Lumbini Garden. (Note: Some traditions observe the birth on the 7th day of the month).
* Enlightenment: The anniversary of his attainment of Buddhahood at Bodh Gaya at the age of 35.
* Parinirvana: The anniversary of his passing into Nirvana at Kushinagar at the age of 81.
* Timing: While the entire month is holy, the 15th day of the 4th Tibetan month is the most important date.
3. Spiritual Practices and Activities
It is taught that during this month, the merit from any virtuous deed is multiplied one hundred thousand times. Consequently, practitioners diligently engage in the following activities:
* Vegetarianism: Abstaining from meat and following a vegetarian diet for the entire month.
* Life Release: Saving the lives of animals—such as fish or birds—destined for slaughter and setting them free.
* Offerings and Circumambulation: Making offerings at temples and circumambulating (walking around) holy stupas and shrines.
* Almsgiving: Practicing generosity by giving to the poor and needy.

daily_calendar_english.Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
daily_calendar_english.Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Sengé Dradrok
On the tenth day of the sheep month, in India’s southern reaches,
You grind to dust the tīrthikas, along with their gods and guardians,
And hoist aloft the victory banner of Buddha’s teaching
Mighty Senge Dradok, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that all obstacles are pacified, longevity, prosperity and perfect qualities are increased,
We gain control over all perceptions, utterly vanquish all opponents,
We are victorious over the four maras, and actualize the benefits of generosity
Towards all beings in the three worlds.

Manifesting as the incomparable human master, Jamyang Khyentse Wangpo,

To you I pray! Bless my mind and inspire my understanding!

daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Tsokyé Dorje
On the tenth day of the monkey month, on the Dhanakośa lake,
In the heart of a wondrous, naturally appearing lotus you are born,
And turn the wheel of Dharma for the island dākinīs—
Guru Tsokyé Dorje, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that all obstructions from illness and döns, and all negative circumstances and obstacles are pacified,
Long life, merit and splendour increase, experiences, realization and clairvoyance arise,
And we are reborn on the Mountain of Glory to look upon your face, Guru, hear your teaching.
And become the son or daughter of your heart.

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: The Holy Anniversary of Guru Rinpoche’s Birthday
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Greetings on the Occasion of the Holy Anniversary of Guru Rinpoche’s Birth
The tenth day of the fifth Tibetan month (the first month of the Monkey Year/season) is a special and extraordinary festival marking the birth of the Great Master Padmasambhava, known as Lake-Born Vajra (Tsokyey Dorje), within the lotus ocean.
1. Significance of the Festival
 * The Actual Birth Anniversary: On the tenth day of the Monkey Month, the Great Master emanated as a beam of light from the Western Pure Land of Sukhavati. He was miraculously born within a lotus blossom in the Dhanakosha Lake of Oddiyana.
 * A Time of Great Blessing: It is traditionally believed that any virtuous or meritorious deeds performed on this sacred day are multiplied a hundred thousand times.
2. Recommended Spiritual Practices
In connection with this festival, it is beneficial for all devotees to engage in the following activities:
 * Guru Yoga Meditation: Visualizing Guru Rinpoche above the crown of one's head and receiving his profound blessings.
 * Recitation of the Vajra Guru Mantra: Chanting as many repetitions as possible of the mantra: Om Ah Hum Vajra Guru Padma Siddhi Hum.
 * Offering Feast Puja (Tsok): Performing the Ganachakra (Feast Offering) based on the two stages of the path (Generation and Completion).
 * Reciting Prayers: Reciting the Sampa Lhundrup (Spontaneous Fulfillment of Wishes) and Barchey Lamsel (Clearing Obstacles from the Path).
> A Dedication Prayer:
> Through the blessings of the Second Buddha, the Great Master Padmasambhava, may there be peace in the world, and may the cause of the Tibetan people's religion and politics swiftly find its truthful resolution.

daily_calendar_english.2nd Event: The Day of the Universal Incense Offering (Dzamling Spyi Sang)
daily_calendar_english.2nd Event's Details:
In the 8th century, after the completion of the Samye Mi-gyur Lhun-gyi Drupa temple (Samye Monastery), Guru Padmasambhava subdued the fierce spirits and deities of Tibet. This day commemorates the occasion when he performed an incense offering (Sang) to all the local deities and guardians of the entire world.
The Purpose: It is observed for the sake of world peace, to create harmony between gods, spirits, and humans, and to clear away all obstacles.

The source of all happiness and benefit without exception,

Is the Lord of Compassion, Tenzin Gyatso.

May your lotus feet remain steadfast until the end of existence.

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Chokhor Duchen (The Festival of Turning the Wheel of Dharma)
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
The Significance of the Festival
The name "Chokhor Duchen" refers to the "Festival of Turning the Wheel of Dharma." This is the day marking the very first time Buddha Shakyamuni turned the Wheel of Dharma (gave his first teachings).
Historical Origin
After Buddha Shakyamuni attained complete enlightenment at Bodh Gaya, India, he remained silent without teaching for seven weeks. Following this, at the request of the gods Brahma and Indra, he traveled to the Deer Park in Varanasi on the 4th day of the 6th Tibetan month. There, he first turned the "Wheel of Dharma of the Four Noble Truths" for his five first disciples and others.

On the tenth day of the bird month, the tīrthikas of Zangling,
Fling you into the river Ganges, but you dance in the sky, and reverse the current.
When the whole land is in peril, and they beg you for refuge and protection, you bring them all within the Dharma
Guru Khanding Tsal, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that all chronic and immune system diseases are healed, our helpers grow in number, and our wishes are fulfilled,
Food and resources of every kind increase, as well as everything we need,
Our mind is peaceful, our speech blazes with power,
And our samādhi meditation becomes sublime and supreme.

daily_calendar_english.Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
daily_calendar_english.Element Meaning: Productive cycle - Earth produces Metal. Favorable for wealth, investments, financial planning.

daily_calendar_english.Auspicious Day's Short Desc: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
daily_calendar_english.Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Dorjé Tötreng Tsal
On the tenth day of the pig month, the gods and demons of Nepal
Are subjugated by Vajrakīla, and then through accomplishing Yangdak
You attain the vidyādhara stage of supreme mahāmudrā
Dorje Tötreng Tsal, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that all illnesses and harm caused by yamas, mamos and jungpos,
Sudden accidents, and untimely death all are eliminated,
We are freed from dangerous curses, gods and demons obey whatever their commands,
And our own bodies become indestructible as vajra.

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Birthday of His Holiness Dudjom Yangsi Tenzin Yeshe Dorje Rinpoche.
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Brief Long-Life Prayer for Kyabje Dudjom Yangsi Tenzin Yeshi Dorjee Rinpoche.
Rig-kün khyab-dag dö-mä gön-po dang
To the Primordial Protector, the All-pervading Lord of all Buddha families,
Yer-me Tän-dzin Ye-she Dor-je chog
Inseparable from the supreme Tenzin Yeshe Dorje,
Sang-sum dor-je'i tri la zhab-tän nä
May your secret Three Vajras (Body, Speech, and Mind) remain firm upon the Vajra Throne,
Thrin-lä nam-khä tha la khyab gyur chig
And may your enlightened activities pervade the furthest reaches of space!

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Parinirvana of H.H. Kathok Gertse Rinpoche
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Supplication to the 4th Katok Getse Rinpoche
Master of the entire Doctrine of the Buddha, the Katokpa,
Upholder of the Victory Banner of the Teachings with Unchanging Activity,
Crown Ornament of millions of scholars and realized masters, the Getsepa,
To the Fearless Lord of Speech, I pray!
This beautiful supplication (gsol 'debs) for the 4th H.H. Katok Getse Rinpoche, Gyurme Tenpa Gyaltsen, was composed by the H.H. Kyabje Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Birthday of Kyabje Kathok Situ Rinpoche
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Long Life Prayer for the Supreme Katok Situ Rinpoche

OM SWASTI | NGE DÖN CHÖ KYI DRÖN ME TONG BAR WAY
Om Swasti! Through the thousand-fold radiance of the Lamp of Definitive Dharma,
 ZHING DIR SHAY DRUB NANG WAY GO GYA JAY
You open a hundred doors of teaching and practice within this realm.
TRIN LAY KÜN TU KHYAB DAK NIR MA KA
All-pervading Lord of enlightened activities, Nirmaka (Emanation),
PAL DEN ZANG PO ZHAB PAY TAK TEN SOL
Glorious and Excellent One, may your lotus feet remain forever firm

Long Life Prayer (Zhabten) for the current incarnation of H.E.Katok Situ Rinpoche, composed by the late Great Master Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche

daily_calendar_english.Notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab
daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Padmasambhava
On the tenth day of the rat month, you tame the gods and spirits of Tibet,
You build Samyé to perfection, and light the flame of the sacred Dharma,
You ripen and liberate your fortunate disciples, the king and subjects
Padmasambhava, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that idle chatter, harsh words, lies and slander,
Impairments of the śrāvaka’ vinaya discipline and bodhisattva precepts,
Breakages of samaya vows and the like, wrongdoing and downfalls, all are healed,
And our whole being is transformed and purified.
daily_calendar_english.Element Meaning: Productive cycle - Metal enriches Water. Good for clarity, communication, strategic planning.

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: Lhabab Duchen: The Festival of the Buddha's Descent from Heaven
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Lhabab Duchen is one of the four great festivals (Chotrul Duchen, Saga Dawa, Chokhor Duchen, and Lhabab Duchen) in the Buddhist calendar.
1. The Essence of the Festival
Lhabab Duchen falls on the 22nd day of the 9th Tibetan month. It commemorates the occasion when Buddha Shakyamuni descended back to Earth in the city of Sankissa (Salden).
The Buddha had traveled to the Heaven of the Thirty-Three (Trāyastriṃśa) to give teachings to the gods, most notably to his mother, Mayadevi, to liberate her from cyclic existence and repay her kindness. After spending the summer retreat (Vassa) there, he returned to the human realm.
2. The Spiritual Benefits (Merit)
According to Buddhist tradition, the effects of both positive and negative actions are immensely multiplied on this day:
 * 100 Million-Fold Merit: It is believed that the merit from any virtuous deed performed on this day is multiplied 100,000,000 times.
 * Purification of Negativity: It is an exceptionally powerful time to practice purification rituals to cleanse past negative karma and downfalls.
3. Traditional Practices: What to do on this day?
To increase their roots of virtue, devotees traditionally engage in the following practices:
 * Nyungne (Fasting): Taking the Mahayana Eight Precepts, which involves a one-day vow of eating only vegetarian food and, in some cases, maintaining silence.
 * Offerings: Lighting butter lamps at temples and stupas, and performing circumambulations (Kora).
 * Recitations: Chanting praises of the Buddha’s miracles and reciting the King of Prayers (Samantabhadra Wishing Prayer).
 * Altar Preparations: Setting up elaborate offerings and white tormas (ritual cakes) in home altars to commemorate the Buddha's descent.

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: The Nine Bad Omens (Ngenpa Gu Dzom)"
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
The Nine Bad Omens (Ngenpa Gu Dzom)
Although there are several different interpretations of how to identify the Nine Bad Omens, according to the traditional systems of Tibetan astrology, they are generally categorized into three groups based on the stars in the sky, the elements on the earth, and the directions in space.
1. The Three Bad Omens of the Sky
These three bad omens arise from the alignment of the planets and the constellations:
* Bad Planets: Regardless of whether the day is a Thursday or a Friday (usually considered auspicious), it is calculated as an unfavorable planetary day where activities will not succeed.
* Bad Constellations: An unfavorable constellation from among the 28 lunar mansions falls on this day.
* Bad Timing: Certain periods or hours of that day are considered inauspicious for any kind of activity.
2. The Three Bad Omens of the Earth
These relate to the elements of the ground and the cardinal directions:
* Incompatible Elements: The elements—Earth, Water, Fire, and Air—clash with one another (for example, Fire and Water meeting).
* Bad Directions: The alignment points toward directions where the "Gates of Heaven" or "Gates of Earth" are said to be open (traditionally seen as risky or unstable).
* Hostile Earth-Lords: The local spirits and "Earth-Lords" (Sa-dak) are considered agitated, making it unsuitable to perform any earthworks or stonework.
3. The Three Intermediate Bad Omens
These refer to the connection between humans and their intended actions:
* Lack of Progress: It is considered an unlucky time for travel, business, or commerce.
* Broken Interdependence: Auspicious ceremonies like weddings or celebrations are likely to face obstacles or bad luck.
* Unmet Intentions: Even if one has good intentions, the results are expected to be poor or unsuccessful.
In Summary
According to the Tibetan calendar, the 6th day of the 11th Tibetan month is the actual day of "Ngenpa Gu Dzom." On this day, Tibetan people avoid starting important work, stay home to rest, and refrain from offering ceremonial scarves (khata)

daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Meditation is not about trying to achieve something. It is about letting go of everything.

— Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: The Ten Auspicious Omens (Zangpo Chu Dzom)
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
The Ten Auspicious Omens (Zangpo Chu Dzom)
In Tibetan astrology, Zangpo Chu Dzom is considered an extremely virtuous and positive day. It falls on the 7th day of the 11th Tibetan month, which is the day immediately following "Ngenpa Gu Dzom" (The Nine Bad Omens).
Below are the detailed explanations and benefits of this day:
1. What are the Ten Auspicious Omens?
The term "Ten Auspicious Omens" refers to the gathering of ten harmonious conditions involving gods, spirits (Nagas), humans, elements, and planets. They are categorized as follows:
* The Three Good Omens of the Sky: (Auspicious planets, auspicious constellations, and auspicious timing.)
* The Three Good Omens of the Earth: (Auspicious directions, harmonious elements, and pleased Earth-Lords.)
* The Three Intermediate Good Omens: (Perfect interdependent connections, fulfillment of intentions, and favorable circumstances for tasks.)
* The One General Auspicious Condition: (The harmonious connection between gods and humans.)
2. Benefits and Virtues of This Day
It is said that any activity started on the day of the Ten Auspicious Omens will succeed without obstacles.
* Success in Activities: Important tasks such as business ventures, marriages, and building new houses are blessed and will reach a successful conclusion.
* Increase of Merit: Performing virtuous deeds and making offerings on this day is believed to multiply one's merit manifold.
* Clearing of Negativity: It is said that the "pollution" and negativity from the previous day (Ngenpa Gu Dzom) are purified, allowing auspicious glory to shine.
* Physical and Mental Well-being: It is a positive time when people naturally feel more harmonious and develop virtuous thoughts.
3. Special Customs and Activities
Tibetan people traditionally observe this day with specific activities:
* Offering Khata: Giving ceremonial scarves as a symbol of auspicious beginnings and interdependence.
* Lhasol and Sang-sol: Offering prayers to local deities and performing smoke purification (Sang) ceremonies.
* Starting New Ventures: If there is a new project to begin, this day is specifically chosen for its favorable energy.
* Celebrations: Families and relatives gather to enjoy festive meals and celebrate together.
In Summary
The transition from the bad timing of "Ngenpa Gu Dzom" to the auspicious timing of "Zangpo Chu Dzom" is viewed as a symbolic event, much like "the sun rising after a period of thick darkness."

On the tenth day of the tiger month, you arrive at the palace in Uḍḍiyāna,
And are adopted by Indrabhūti as his crown prince.
Taking Prabhāvatī as your wife, you rule as a universal monarch—
Guru Pema Gyalpo, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that our brilliance, charisma and strength increase, epidemics and turmoil in the elements are pacified,
The five senses become pliable, and happiness, peace of mind and confidence quickly arise,
We gather under our power the prāṇa-mind of humans and non-humans,
And all our aspirations are fulfilled, just as we desire.

daily_calendar_english.1st Event's image: Birthday. of Kyabje Yangshi Dungse Gyana ta Rinpoche.PNG
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
Long Life Prayer for the Dungse Gyana Ta Reincarnation of Kyabje Dungse Thinley Norbu Rinpoche

OM SWASTI | TSE LHA RIG DZIN GYA TSOY NGO WO NYID 
Om Swasti! You are the very essence of the ocean of Longevity Deities and Vidyadharas.
CHI ME TSO KYE DOR JE JIN LAP KYI 
Through the blessings of the Deathless Lake-Born Vajra (Guru Rinpoche),
DUNG SE JNA NA KAL GYAR ZHAB TEN CHING May the lotus feet of Dungse Jnana remain firm for hundreds of eons,
LAP CHEN TEN DROY DZAY PA THAR CHIN SHOK 
And may your vast enlightened activities for the teachings and beings be perfectly fulfilled!
~ At the persistent request of Lama Ösel Dorje Namgyal Wangdu, this prayer was written by one who bears the name of the Dudjom Tulku, Sangye Pema Zhepa. It was composed on the 10th day of the Tibetan month in the Wood-Horse year of the 17th Rabjung cycle. May these aspirations be meaningfully realized

daily_calendar_english.1st Event: The Great Nyingma Prayer Festival (Nyingma Monlam Chenmo) in BodhGaya.
daily_calendar_english.1st Event's Details:
The Nyingma Monlam Chenmo at Bodh Gaya is a major religious festival held annually in front of the Holy Mahabodhi Stupa in Bodh Gaya, India. Its primary purpose is to pray for world peace and the flourishing of the Buddha's teachings.
Key details about this event include:
* Timing: Traditionally, it is held for ten days, from the 1st to the 10th day of the 12th Tibetan month.
* Location: It takes place under the Bodhi Tree at Bodh Gaya, the supreme holy site where Shakyamuni Buddha attained complete enlightenment.
* Objective: To offer prayers for world peace, the well-being of all sentient beings, and specifically for the preservation and spread of the Early Translation School (Nyingma) teachings.
* Leadership: High lamas of the Nyingma lineage, Khenpos, Tulkus, and thousands of monks and nuns gather together for the ceremony.
What is primarily recited?
During this prayer festival, the main recitation is "The King of Aspiration Prayers: Samantabhadra’s Sevenfold Prayer" (Phagpa Zangpo Chöpay Mönlam Gyi Gyalpo). In addition to this, many other supplications and aspiration prayers from the lineage of the Awareness-Holders (Vidyadharas) are recited.
Significance for your projects
Given your involvement in creating booklets and coordinating with lamas for sacred ceremonies (such as the Guru Rinpoche Tsok Chöd), this festival is the pinnacle of such spiritual gatherings. The text you previously mentioned, "Kalsang Dö Jö", is perfectly aligned with the spirit of these Great Prayer Festivals.

daily_calendar_english.Day Significane:
Guru Śāntarakṣita
On the tenth day of the hare month, driven by circumstances and by signs,
You relinquish the kingdom for the Chilly Grove charnel ground.
There, through your yogic disciplines of union and liberation, you make every connection of the highest meaning
Śāntarakṣita, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that our power, position and wealth increase, all human beings are attracted,
All living things are magnetized, earth lords obey us like servants,
We see good dreams and auspicious signs,
And burst open the treasure of siddhis, ordinary and supreme.
