core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Guru Dechen Nyima Özer
On the tenth day of the dog month, the tīrthikas give you poison,
Unharmed, you transform it into nectar, and shine in splendour.
They and all their followers are inspired with faith and enter the teaching of Buddha
Guru of Great Bliss, Nyima Özer, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that obstacles of the year, month, day and time as well as ill omens are pacified,
All obstacles in the way of the Dharma and liberation are dispelled,
Any decline in our personal power and lungta is repaired
And we are victorious in every direction.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
ཁྱི་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་མུ་སྟེགས་དུག་སྦྱོར་གྱིས། །
མ་ཚུགས་བདུད་རྩིར་བསྒྱུར་ནས་གཟི་མདངས་འབར། །
འཁོར་བཅས་དད་ཐོབ་ནང་གི་བསྟན་ལ་བཅུག །
བདེ་ཆེན་ཉི་མ་འོད་ཟེར་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
ལོ་ཟླ་ཞག་དུས་ཀེག་དང་ལྟས་ངན་ཞི། །
ཆོས་དང་ཐར་པའི་བར་དུ་གཅོད་པ་སེལ། །
དབང་ཐང་ཀླུང་རྟ་གུད་སོགས་སོར་ཆུད་ཅིང༌། །
ཕྱོགས་ལས་རྣམ་པར་རྒྱལ་བར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Guru Dorjé Tötreng Tsal
On the tenth day of the pig month, the gods and demons of Nepal
Are subjugated by Vajrakīla, and then through accomplishing Yangdak
You attain the vidyādhara stage of supreme mahāmudrā
Dorje Tötreng Tsal, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that all illnesses and harm caused by yamas, mamos and jungpos,
Sudden accidents, and untimely death all are eliminated,
We are freed from dangerous curses, gods and demons obey whatever their commands,
And our own bodies become indestructible as vajra.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
ཕག་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་བལ་ཡུལ་ལྷ་འདྲེ་རྣམས། །
ཕུར་བུས་བཏུལ་ནས་ཡང་དག་སྒྲུབ་པ་མཛད། །
ཕྱག་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་མཆོག་གི་རིག་འཛིན་གྲུབ། །
རྡོ་རྗེ་ཐོད་ཕྲེང་རྩལ་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
གཤིན་རྗེ་མ་མོ་འབྱུང་པོའི་ནད་དང་གདོན། །
གློ་བུར་ཡེ་འདྲོག་དུས་མིན་འཆི་བ་ཞི། །
གཏད་སེལ་ལས་གྲོལ་ལྷ་འདྲེས་ཅི་བཅོལ་ཉན། །
རང་ལུས་རྡོ་རྗེར་འགྱུར་བར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Guru Padmasambhava
On the tenth day of the rat month, you tame the gods and spirits of Tibet,
You build Samyé to perfection, and light the flame of the sacred Dharma,
You ripen and liberate your fortunate disciples, the king and subjects
Padmasambhava, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that idle chatter, harsh words, lies and slander,
Impairments of the śrāvaka’ vinaya discipline and bodhisattva precepts,
Breakages of samaya vows and the like, wrongdoing and downfalls, all are healed,
And our whole being is transformed and purified.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
བྱི་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་བོད་ཡུལ་ལྷ་འདྲེ་བཏུལ། །
བསམ་ཡས་ལེགས་བཞེངས་དམ་ཆོས་སྒྲོན་མེ་སྦར། །
སྐལ་ལྡན་རྗེ་འབངས་སྨིན་ཅིང་གྲོལ་བར་མཛད། །
པདྨ་སཾ་བྷ་བ་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
ངག་འཁྱལ་ཚིག་རྩུབ་རྫུན་སྨྲས་སྐུར་པ་བཏབ། །
ཉན་ཐོས་འདུལ་ཁྲིམས་བྱང་སེམས་བསླབ་པ་ཉམས། །
གསང་སྔགས་དམ་ཚིག་འགལ་སོགས་ཉེས་ལྟུང་རྣམས། །
བྱང་ཞིང་བདག་རྒྱུད་དག་པར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Guru Dorjé Drolö
On the tenth day of the ox month, master and disciples confer,
And in the centre and on the borders of the land of snows you conceal millions of terma troves.
With these treasures you safeguard what remains of the teachings, all with your constant kindness
All-powerful Dorje Drolö, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that sudden misfortune, like untimely death, never befalls us,
And as soon as we transfer to the next life, we are born in the pure realm of Great Bliss,
And in the paradise of Lotus Light, in your very presence, Guru,
And become a source of limitless benefit to living beings.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Guru Pema Gyalpo
On the tenth day of the tiger month, you arrive at the palace in Uḍḍiyāna,
And are adopted by Indrabhūti as his crown prince.
Taking Prabhāvatī as your wife, you rule as a universal monarch—
Guru Pema Gyalpo, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that our brilliance, charisma and strength increase, epidemics and turmoil in the elements are pacified,
The five senses become pliable, and happiness, peace of mind and confidence quickly arise,
We gather under our power the prāṇa-mind of humans and non-humans,
And all our aspirations are fulfilled, just as we desire.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
སྟག་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་ཨོ་རྒྱན་རྒྱལ་སར་ཕེབས། །
ཨིནྡྲ་བྷཱུ་ཏིའི་སྲས་ཀྱི་སྐལ་པར་མཛད། །
འོད་འཆང་ཁབ་བཞེས་འཁོར་ལོས་སྒྱུར་རྒྱལ་གྱུར། །
གུ་རུ་པདྨ་རྒྱལ་པོར་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
གཟི་བརྗིད་སྟོབས་རྒྱས་ནད་རིགས་འབྱུང་འཁྲུགས་ཞི། །
དབང་ལྔ་ལས་རུང་ཡིད་བདེ་སྤོབས་པ་མྱུར། །
མི་དང་མི་མིན་རླུང་སེམས་དབང་དུ་འདུས། །
བསམ་དོན་ཡིད་བཞིན་འགྲུབ་པར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to turn away immediately From those things which bring desire and attachment. For the pleasures of the senses are just like salty water: The more we taste of them, the more our thirst increases.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to entertain concepts, Which revolve around dualistic notions of perceiver and perceived, In the knowledge that all these appearances are but the mind itself, Whilst mind’s own nature is forever beyond the limitations of ideas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to let go of grasping When encountering things one finds pleasant or attractive, Considering them to be like rainbows in the summer skies— Beautiful in appearance, yet in truth devoid of any substance.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to recognize delusion Whenever one is confronted by adversity or misfortune. For these sufferings are just like the death of a child in a dream, And it’s so exhausting to cling to delusory perceptions as real.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to give out of generosity, With no hopes of karmic recompense or expectation of reward. For if those who seek awakening must give even their own bodies, What need is there to mention mere outer objects and possessions?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to observe ethical restraint, Without the slightest intention of continuing in saṃsāric existence. For lacking discipline one will never secure even one’s own well-being, And so any thought of bringing benefit to others would be absurd.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
Guru Śāntarakṣita
On the tenth day of the hare month, driven by circumstances and by signs,
You relinquish the kingdom for the Chilly Grove charnel ground.
There, through your yogic disciplines of union and liberation, you make every connection of the highest meaning
Śāntarakṣita, to you we pray.
 Grant your blessing so that our power, position and wealth increase, all human beings are attracted,
All living things are magnetized, earth lords obey us like servants,
We see good dreams and auspicious signs,
And burst open the treasure of siddhis, ordinary and supreme.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
ཡོས་ཟླའི་ཚེས་བཅུར་རྐྱེན་དང་བརྡས་བསྐུལ་ཏེ། །
རྒྱལ་སྲིད་སྤངས་ནས་བསིལ་བའི་ཚལ་དུ་ཕེབས། །
འབྲེལ་ཚད་དོན་ལྡན་སྦྱོར་སྒྲོལ་བརྟུལ་ཞུགས་གྲུབ། །
ཤཱནྟ་རཀྵི་ཏ་ལ་གསོལ་བ་འདེབས། །
མངའ་ཐང་ལོངས་སྤྱོད་འཕེལ་ཞིང་རྐང་འགྲོས་འདུ། །
འགྲོ་ཀུན་དབང་འདུས་གཞི་བདག་བྲན་བཞིན་ཉན། །
རྨི་ལམ་རྟགས་མཚན་བཟང་པོར་མཐོང་བ་དང༌། །
གྲུབ་གཉིས་གཏེར་ཁ་བརྡོལ་བར་བྱིན་གྱིས་རློབས། །
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for wealth practices.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Certainty: Fulfills wishes speedily; resolves disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan Day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

core_eng_tib.Lunar Status: The (Tibetan day) is doubled this month
core_eng_tib.Day Significane: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
core_eng_tib.Unnamed: 19:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.
core_eng_tib.Daily Restriction: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

core_eng_tib.Day Significane:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
core_eng_tib.Meaning of Coincidence: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

core_eng_tib.Day Significane: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.
